name: Heng Huang 0001
subject: Multimodal ML Research Opportunity for B.Tech Student
body: Dear Professor Huang,I am Aditya Srivastava, a second-year B.Tech student in Information Technology at Delhi Technological University, India, ranking in the top 0.4% nationally in JEE Main.I was particularly interested in your recent work on "MIRROR: Multi-Modal Pathological Self-Supervised Representation Learning," which aligns with my experience building a Masked Autoencoder for hyperspectral imagery to learn spatial-spectral representations. My work at ArchiGen AI Labs also involved designing RAG pipelines that improved AI response accuracy, touching upon multimodal integration.I am seeking an opportunity to contribute to your ongoing research as a research intern or assistant, applying my skills in machine learning, computer vision, and foundation models to challenging real-world problems.My CV is attached for your review, and my GitHub (https://github.com/Aditya-Srivastava-01) provides further details on my projects.Sincerely,Aditya Srivastava

name: Bo Han 0003
subject: OOD Detection Research Internship Inquiry
body: Dear Professor Han,I am Aditya Srivastava, a second-year B.Tech student in Information Technology at Delhi Technological University. My work applies machine learning to real-world systems, with a strong interest in out-of-distribution detection. Your paper, 'Provable Discriminative Hyperspherical Embedding for Out-of-Distribution Detection,' aligns directly with my project on Android malware detection. There, I distinguished malicious (OOD) from benign (ID) APKs using a 1,418-dimensional feature space, achieving 90.8% recall through robust classification. I am seeking an opportunity to contribute to your ongoing research as a research intern or assistant, applying machine learning, computer vision, or scientific AI. My CV is attached, and my GitHub is at https://github.com/Aditya-Srivastava-01. Thank you for your time. Aditya Srivastava
papersReferenced: PoP: A Pipeline-oriented Paradigm for Long-tailed Learning Survey
FedGPS: Statistical Rectification Against Data Heterogeneity in Federated Learning
Provable Discriminative Hyperspherical Embedding for Out-of-Distribution Detection
Classifying Long-tailed and Label-noise Data via Disentangling and Unlearning

name: Philip Torr 0001
subject: OOD Detection Research Opportunity
body:
Dear Professor Torr 0001,

I am Aditya Srivastava, a second-year B.Tech student in Information Technology at Delhi Technological University. My work focuses on applying machine learning to challenging real-world systems across various domains.

I am particularly interested in your recent paper, "DEEDEE: Fast and Scalable Out-of-Distribution Dynamics Detection." My experience building an Android Malware Detection Pipeline, which involved reverse-engineering binaries to extract a 1,418-dimensional feature space and benchmarking models for 90.8% malicious recall, gave me practical insight into identifying anomalous patterns in complex data, aligning with the OOD detection challenges you address.

I am writing to inquire about the possibility of contributing to your ongoing research projects as a research intern or research assistant. I am keen to apply my skills in machine learning, computer vision, NLP, or scientific AI to challenging real-world problems within your lab.

My CV is attached for your review, and further details about my work can be found on my GitHub: https://github.com/Aditya-Srivastava-01

Sincerely,
Aditya Srivastava
papersReferenced: Unforgotten Safety: Preserving Safety Alignment of Large Language Models with Continual Learning
Measuring what Matters: Construct Validity in Large Language Model Benchmarks
DEEDEE: Fast and Scalable Out-of-Distribution Dynamics Detection
MATRIX: Multimodal Agent Tuning for Robust Tool-Use Reasoning

name: Mohit Bansal
subject: Research Interest: Agentic AI and Video Perception
body:
Dear Professor Bansal,

I am Aditya Srivastava, a second-year B.Tech student in Information Technology at Delhi Technological University. My academic background includes a 99.59 percentile in JEE Main, and my work applies machine learning to challenging real-world systems.

I am particularly interested in your work on agentic long video understanding, such as "Active Video Perception." My experience building RAG pipelines, which improved AI response accuracy by 30% for enterprise AI systems, aligns with the concept of agents actively seeking and acquiring query-relevant evidence from complex data. I have also developed a hyperspectral foundation model to learn transferable spatial-spectral representations.

I am seeking an opportunity to contribute to one of your ongoing research projects as a research intern or research assistant. I am keen to apply my skills in machine learning, computer vision, and foundation models to real-world problems.

My CV is attached for your review, and my GitHub profile at https://github.com/Aditya-Srivastava-01 provides further examples of my work.

Sincerely,
Aditya Srivastava
papersReferenced: TimeRefine: Temporal Grounding with Time Refining Video LLM
Hierarchy-Aware Multimodal Unlearning for Medical AI
Active Video Perception: Iterative Evidence Seeking for Agentic Long Video Understanding
Prune-Then-Plan: Step-Level Calibration for Stable Frontier Exploration in Embodied Question Answering

name: Mang Ye
subject: Computer Vision Research: Masked Autoencoders
body:
Dear Professor Ye,

I am Aditya Srivastava, a second-year B.Tech student in Information Technology at Delhi Technological University, highly interested in applying machine learning to challenging real-world systems.

My interest in computer vision and self-supervised learning leads me to your research, specifically your 2026 paper, 'Scalable and Generalizable Correspondence Pruning Via Geometry-Consistent Pre-Training'. My own project on a Hyperspectral Foundation Model involved building a Masked Autoencoder to learn transferable spatial-spectral representations, directly aligning with the masked autoencoder pre-training framework described.

I am seeking an opportunity to contribute to one of your ongoing research projects as a research intern or research assistant. I am keen to apply my experience in computer vision, self-supervised learning, and foundation models to real-world problems within your lab.

My CV is attached for your review, and my GitHub profile is available at https://github.com/Aditya-Srivastava-01.

Sincerely,
Aditya Srivastava

name: Jiajun Wu 0001
subject: Computer Vision Research — Contribution Opportunity
body:
Dear Professor Wu,

I am Aditya Srivastava, a second-year B.Tech student in Information Technology at Delhi Technological University, 99.59 percentile in JEE Main, top 0.4% among more than one million candidates nationwide.

Your paper, 'Seeing the Wind from a Falling Leaf', which models motions from videos and recovers invisible forces from visual observations, is particularly interesting to me. This work technically involves an end-to-end differentiable inverse graphics framework to jointly model object geometry, physical properties, and interactions directly from videos. My project on building a Masked Autoencoder (MAE) for hyperspectral imagery to learn transferable spatial-spectral representations shares a connection with your work, as both deal with learning representations from visual data.

I was wondering whether there might be an opportunity to contribute to your ongoing research.

My CV is attached, and further work is available on my GitHub: https://github.com/Aditya-Srivastava-01.
   
Best,
Aditya Srivastava

Your paper, 'PDPMamba: Potential Panoptic Driving Perception via Multi-Task Visual Mamba', employs a framework with an encoder shared by three decoders, using multi-scale visual Mamba to extract feature information. name: Rynson W. H. Lau
subject: Vision-Language Models Research — Contribution Opportunity
body:
Dear Professor Lau,

I am Aditya Srivastava, a second-year B.Tech student in Information Technology at Delhi Technological University, top 0.4% nationally in JEE Main.




Your paper 'Understanding the robustness of vision-language models to medical image artefacts' evaluates VLMs' performance on images with and without artefacts, which technically assesses their robustness through benchmarking on real-world medical datasets.
